title: Disciplinary Process Triggers — HR Case Codes Correlated with Security Incidents

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.6.4 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.6.4 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.19: Disciplinary Process Triggers — HR Case Codes Correlated with Security Incidents.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.19' running on snow:hr_case (restricted), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=hr_restricted sourcetype=snow:hr_case earliest=-180d category=conduct
| eval day=round(_time/86400,0)
| join type=inner day [| search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident category=security earliest=-180d | eval day=round(_time/86400,0) | stats dc(number) as sec_cases by day]
| stats count by day
description: Correlates anonymized HR case creation timestamps with security investigations to evidence disciplinary process alignment without exposing PII in dashboards.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes hr_restricted, itsm and sourcetypes snow:hr_case, snow:incident. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Disciplinary Process Triggers — HR Case Codes Correlated with Security Incidents. ISO 27001 tells us what good security management looks like, and we document that we follow it.
implementation: (1) Strict RBAC on index; (2) legal privacy review; (3) aggregate only; (4) document data minimization. Indexes required: `index=hr_restricted`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:hr_case`, `sourcetype=snow:incident`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: ServiceNow HR case HEC (restricted index).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `snow:hr_case` (restricted).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Strict RBAC on index; (2) legal privacy review; (3) aggregate only; (4) document data minimization.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hr_restricted sourcetype=snow:hr_case earliest=-180d category=conduct
| eval day=round(_time/86400,0)
| join type=inner day [| search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident category=security earliest=-180d | eval day=round(_time/86400,0) | stats dc(number) as sec_cases by day]
| stats count by day
```

Understanding this SPL

**Disciplinary Process Triggers — HR Case Codes Correlated with Security Incidents (A.6.4)** — Correlates anonymized HR case creation timestamps with security investigations to evidence disciplinary process alignment without exposing PII in dashboards.

Documented **Data sources**: `snow:hr_case` (restricted). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): ServiceNow HR case HEC (restricted index). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hr_restricted; **sourcetype**: snow:hr_case. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hr_restricted, sourcetype=snow:hr_case, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **day** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `stats` aggregates events by **day**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.

Output fields: day, day], sec_cases.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Single value
visualization: Time chart, Single value. Key fields: `day`, `day]`, `sec_cases`.
knownFalsePositives: Incidents may bunch during major events, vulnerability bulletin response, or controlled exercises.
